nav.topbarText: KOMA National Platform · Republic of Guinea

hero.badge: National Information Platform · Republic of Guinea
hero.lead: The national digital registry that identifies, maps and mobilizes companies, equipment and strategic projects of the Republic of Guinea — serving investment and local content.

contexte.title: From the Simandou Corridor to structural projects: capitalizing on achievements
contexte.p1: The Simandou Project is one of the largest mining and infrastructure projects in the world. Its implementation required mobilizing national and international companies, heavy equipment, specialized skills and large-scale logistics for the construction of the mine, the Trans-Guinean railway and the mineral port.
contexte.p2: With the progressive completion of certain phases, some of these capacities are becoming available and represent a strategic opportunity to accelerate the Guinean Government's structural projects, particularly in road, rail, port, energy, urban and industrial infrastructure.
contexte.p3: This forum serves as a strategic platform to maximize the economic returns on investments made, preserve acquired expertise, strengthen local content and foster new investment opportunities in Guinea.
contexte.quote: Through this forum, the Government intends to create a framework for consultation, partnership and networking between public, private, financial and technical stakeholders to transform Simandou's achievements into concrete national development drivers.

vision.title: Identify, valorize and remobilize Simandou Project capacities
vision.desc: Establish a platform for dialogue and coordination to identify, valorize and remobilize the equipment, technical expertise and companies that participated in building the Simandou Project infrastructure, with a view to deploying them towards national structural projects.
vision.item1Desc: Conduct an inventory of available equipment, qualified human resources and technical capacities from the Simandou Project.
vision.item2Desc: Facilitate the reallocation of these resources towards the State's priority projects.
vision.item3Desc: Strengthen partnerships between public administrations, national and international companies, and financial institutions.
vision.item4Desc: Define a strategic, contractual and regulatory framework enabling an effective and sustainable transition.
vision.item5Desc: Promote local content, national subcontracting and the development of Guinean companies.
vision.item6Desc: Align public, private, financial and technical actors around major national structural projects.

impacts.title: Concrete commitments in service of national development
impacts.item1: Consolidated database of available equipment, priority project needs and the capacities of participating companies.
impacts.item2: Agreements, MoUs, partnerships and letters of intent promoting equipment reallocation and collaboration between local and international companies.
impacts.item3: Operational remobilization plan detailing actions, responsibilities and implementation timelines.
impacts.item4: Concrete recommendations and monitoring mechanism to ensure an effective transition of equipment, companies and jobs towards national projects.
impacts.item5: Acceleration of the implementation of the State's priority infrastructure projects.
impacts.item6: Strengthening of local content and Guinean national subcontracting.
impacts.item7: Creation of a national observatory to monitor investments and available capacities.

composantes.title: Choose your registry, register
composantes.desc: Each component has a «Why?» page detailing its role. The Projects components also provide access to available offers.
composantes.comp1desc: Census of companies present on the national territory and their technical capacities.
composantes.comp1whyIntro: This component constitutes the first national registry of technical capacities. It identifies available expertise, strengthens national business participation, and facilitates connections between project holders and companies.
comp1whyList: RCCM — Trade and Mobile Credit Register
composantes.comp1regIntro: Select the type of entity you represent to access the corresponding registration form.
composantes.comp2desc: Census of industrial, mining, railway and civil engineering equipment available for mobilization.
composantes.comp2whyIntro: By listing available equipment, this component optimizes their use, reduces mobilization costs, and enhances a strategic industrial fleet for major project implementation.
composantes.comp2regIntro: Select the category of equipment you wish to register in the national inventory.
composantes.comp3desc: National registry of all public investment projects, classified by strategic sector.
composantes.comp3whyIntro: This module lists all state public investment projects. It serves as a basis for planning, inter-ministerial coordination, and connection with the technical capacities and equipment inventoried.
comp3whyList: Infrastructure — roads, bridges, highways, ports, airports, railways
Energy — dams, power plants, rural electrification
Health — hospitals, health centers, biomedical equipment
composantes.comp3regIntro: Select the sector of your public project to access the dedicated registration form.

comp3regItems.label: Infrastructure
comp3regItems.sub: Roads · bridges · highways · ports · airports · railways

composantes.comp4desc: Registry of major structural projects led by the private sector on the national territory.
composantes.comp4whyIntro: This component lists structural projects from the private sector to articulate them with public policies, mobilize national capacities, and foster synergies between investors and local businesses.
composantes.comp4regIntro: Select the nature of your structural private project to access the corresponding form.

themes.description: Presentation of major infrastructure completed (railway corridor, deep-water port, rail line) and identification of national capacities mobilizable for future structuring projects.
Presentation of national priorities for infrastructure and major projects included in Guinea Vision 2040.
Government priority projects and opportunities for companies that participated in the Simandou project to reposition on new markets.
Strategic dialogue between Government, financial institutions and technical partners to align financing with national priority projects.
Detailed presentation of road infrastructure projects in the 2040 program, with the Ministries of Infrastructure and Transport.
Workshops on railway and port projects, in collaboration with the TransGuinean Company (CTG) and relevant ministries.
Energy, hydraulic and sanitation infrastructure projects included in the national development program 2040.
Urban planning, housing, land use and social infrastructure: Guinea Vision 2040 projects presented to private sector actors.

agenda.titre: Plenary Session 3 — Presentation of the Investor Journey
Panel 1 — Enterprise Remobilization Opportunities on Priority Projects
agenda.description: Moderator: Mr. Yaya SOW, Infrastructure Advisor to the Presidency. Panelists: Ms. Yariatou CAMARA (DG DGPIP) · Mr. Amadou DIALLO (Min. Infrastructure) · Mr. Moïse SIDIBÉ (DG AGEROUTE) · Mr. Ibrahima Abé DIALLO (ACGP) · Mr. Mamadou Angelo DIALLO (Delivery Unit) · Mr. Aly KABA (CEO GPC).

agenda.titre: Panel 2 — Aligning Financing with National Priorities and Market Opportunities
agenda.description: Moderator: Dr Mohamed Lamine DOUMBOUYA, MAMRI Coordinator. Panelists: Ms. Maïmouna DIAKHABY (Advisor to the Presidency) · Mr. Aboubacar DIAKITE (DNF, Min. Economy) · Mr. Alpha Boubacar DIALLO (IDB) · Mr. Diawadou BARRY (APB) · Mr. Moussa II KAMISSOKO (BNIG) · Mr. Issa DIAW (World Bank) · Mr. ZANKLI Laté Dodji LAWSON (AfDB).
Moderator: Karim BARRY · Presenter: DG BSD Ministry of Infrastructure · Priority road sections and portfolio under the Simandou 2040 program.
Moderator: Ibrahima Ahmed BARRY · Facilitators: DG PAC / DG SNCFG / DG ANAIM · Strategic rail corridors and modernization of Guinean ports.
Moderator: Aissatou SANÉ · Facilitators: SG Ministry of Energy · SG Ministry of Sanitation, Hydraulics and Hydrocarbons.
Moderator: Nana DIABY · Facilitator: DG BSD MUHAT · Urban roads, sanitation, public buildings and public facilities.
Matchmaking sessions between companies, project owners, investors and public institutions.
Memoranda of Understanding (MoU) · Framework Agreements · Letters of Intent · Partnership Agreements.
Presentation and adoption of the Forum's main recommendations.
Closing speech by the Prime Minister or his representative.

faq.question: Who can participate in the Remobilization Forum?
faq.reponse: The Forum is open to all economic, institutional and civil society actors: national and international companies, government representatives, technical and financial partners, press and media.

faq.question: Where will the forum take place?
faq.reponse: The Simandou 2040 Remobilization Forum will be held in Conakry, Republic of Guinea. The exact address and access instructions will be communicated to registered participants.

faq.question: How do I get my access badge?
faq.reponse: After your online registration, you will receive a confirmation email with your badge number. Present this number at reception on the day of the event to collect your physical badge.

faq.question: Is the forum free to attend?
faq.reponse: Registration for the Forum is free for participants. Certain VIP spaces and exhibitor packages are subject to specific fees. Contact us for more information.

faq.question: Are there networking opportunities?
faq.reponse: Yes, the Forum includes structured networking sessions, shared lunch breaks and a dedicated lounge for B2B meetings between companies and investors.

faq.question: How do I become an exhibitor or sponsor?
faq.reponse: Use the dedicated forms available on this page (Sponsors and Exhibitors sections) or contact our team directly. Different packages are available depending on your objectives.
